config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena kalau tidak, minoritas dan orang yang lemah mudah ditindas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan tetap menghormati kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum bagi tiap orang. Saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically come before the individual, because in the United States we have seen how “the common good” can be used to excuse abuses of power and the trampling of minority rights. Public safety and social order matter, but they only have legitimacy when they are limited by constitutional rights, due process, and equal protection. If society can override individual rights whenever it claims a collective interest, then those rights are not real protections at all. The better principle is that society’s interests matter, but they must be pursued within firm boundaries that protect the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju jika kepentingan masyarakat ditempatkan di atas hak individu secara mutlak. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan tekanan terhadap minoritas, orang kecil, atau mereka yang suaranya lemah. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, hukum yang adil, dan perlindungan yang nyata agar tidak berubah menjadi alat penindasan. Jadi, bagi saya, yang benar bukan mendahulukan masyarakat atas individu, melainkan mencari keseimbangan yang tegas tanpa mengorbankan martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to justify coercion, censorship, and discrimination in the name of the "greater good." That does not mean individual rights are absolute in every situation, since things like public health, safety, and fraud laws do require limits, but those limits have to be narrow, justified, and subject to real checks. When people say society comes first, I worry they are giving too much power to whoever gets to define "society" in the first place. To me, a free society is strongest when it protects individual rights first and then builds the common good around them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat dinamika di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan kelompok kecil, membungkam suara yang berbeda, atau membenarkan tindakan yang tidak adil. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang perlu pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat terbatas, jelas dasarnya, dan diawasi ketat. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” mudah berubah menjadi alat kekuasaan, bukan benar-benar kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that logic has repeatedly been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. I do not deny that public health, safety, and law enforcement sometimes require limits, but those limits should be narrow, clearly justified, and subject to strong constitutional checks. The problem with putting "society" first is that it is usually powerful people who decide what society wants, and minorities or unpopular individuals pay the price. A healthy society is not one that can override rights at will; it is one that protects individual liberty while pursuing the common good within firm boundaries.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
